title: senml profiles.py: location() accepts invalid lat/lon without validation
description:
File: python/src/lichen/senml/profiles.py
Lines: 31-51

The location() function accepts lat and lon parameters without any validation:
- No range check: lat must be [-90, 90], lon must be [-180, 180]
- No NaN/Inf check: isinf() and isnan() values would produce invalid geographic data

This is the Python implementation; similar bug project-LICHEN-71ta covers the C implementation in senml.c.

Impact:
- Invalid coordinates silently encoded as valid SenML records
- Downstream consumers expecting valid WGS-84 coordinates may fail
- Interop issues with stricter SenML implementations

Fix: Add validation before creating records:
  import math
  if math.isnan(lat) or math.isnan(lon) or math.isinf(lat) or math.isinf(lon):
      raise ValueError('lat/lon cannot be NaN or Inf')
  if not (-90.0 <= lat <= 90.0):
      raise ValueError(f'lat {lat} out of range [-90, 90]')
  if not (-180.0 <= lon <= 180.0):
      raise ValueError(f'lon {lon} out of range [-180, 180]')
close_reason: Added input validation for lat/lon per spec; tests and linters pass
